texto: What kind of a world do we live in that has room for dog yoga but not for Esperanto?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Arika Okrent que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “What kind of a world do we live in that has room for dog yoga but not for Esperanto?”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, dogs, esperanto, com foco em esperanto e injustice.

texto: When one heated exchange (in English) led a commenter to write "Go fuck yourself!" in Lojban, it turned into a lengthy discussion of why he hadn't said what he meant to say, and what the proper Lojban expression for the sentiment might be.
explicacao: Citação de Arika Okrent sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “When one heated exchange (in English) led a commenter to write "Go fuck yourself!" in Lojban, it …” articula humor, humorous, language em registro de humor, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e language.

texto: The job of the linguist, like that of the biologist or the botanist, is not to tell us how nature should behave, or what its creations should look like, but to describe those creations in all their messy glory and try to figure out what they can teach us about life, the world, and, especially in the case of linguistics, the workings of the human mind.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Arika Okrent: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “The job of the linguist, like that of the biologist or the botanist, is not to tell us how nature…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, language, linguistics pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e linguistics.

texto: Before you judge me as some kind of 'anything goes' language heathen, let me just say that I'm not against usage standards. I don't violate them when I want to sound like an educated person, for the same reason I don't wear a bikini to a funeral when I want to look like a respectful person. There are social conventions for the way we do lots of things, and it is to everyone's benefit to be familiar with them. But logic ain't got nothin' to do with it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Arika Okrent: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Before you judge me as some kind of 'anything goes' language heathen, let me just say that I'm no…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, arika-okrent, language pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de language e linguistics.

There are social conventions for the way we do lots of things, and it is to everyone's benefit to be familiar with them. But logic ain't got nothin' to do with it.

texto: How many Lojbanists does it take to change a brokenlight-bulb?” goes the old Lojban joke. “Two: one to decidewhat to change it into and one to decide what kind of bulbemits broken light.
